title: RS Hopper
wikitext:
The '''RS Hoppers'''<ref>The name of the units is revealed in a [[:File:Eva3-33 C1183 rshopper.jpg|monitor display]] after all four are destroyed.</ref> are four mobile drones seen in [[Evangelion 3.0]] that service [[Evangelion Unit 13]].

The hoppers are initially stored inside containers attached to Eva-13's shoulder pylons and are released when [[Evangelion Unit-02|Eva-02]] attacks using its A.T. Field. Each unit deploys it own A.T. Field both defensively and offensively. The movements of the drones appears to be mostly automatic, but they seem to respond to the pilot's actions or emotions as well.

When deployed, the hoppers appear as vaguely elliptical floating drones. Their bodies are composed of an unidentified semi-translucent material with several detached light blue tendrils trailing behind them. These tendrils billow and contract behind the hoppers as they move, similar to the tentacles of a jellyfish. At the centre of the body of each drone is a circular metal object, similar to the core block of a [[Evangelion Mark.04]] drone. The hoppers take on a more mechanical form similar to rounded cylinders when deploying an AT Field, as well as shortly before their defeat. 

All four units are ultimately destroyed by Eva-02. When killed, they explode violently despite their small size.

[[Mari]] later notes that Eva-13 does not have an [[AT Field]]. The purpose of the hoppers may be to remedy this deficiency. Eva-13 does not deploy any AT Field of its own, and any seen are solely due to the hoppers.

html:
The RS Hoppers[1] are four mobile drones seen in Evangelion 3.0 that service Evangelion Unit 13.

The hoppers are initially stored inside containers attached to Eva-13's shoulder pylons and are released when Eva-02 attacks using its A.T. Field. Each unit deploys it own A.T. Field both defensively and offensively. The movements of the drones appears to be mostly automatic, but they seem to respond to the pilot's actions or emotions as well.

When deployed, the hoppers appear as vaguely elliptical floating drones. Their bodies are composed of an unidentified semi-translucent material with several detached light blue tendrils trailing behind them. These tendrils billow and contract behind the hoppers as they move, similar to the tentacles of a jellyfish. At the centre of the body of each drone is a circular metal object, similar to the core block of a Evangelion Mark.04 drone. The hoppers take on a more mechanical form similar to rounded cylinders when deploying an AT Field, as well as shortly before their defeat.

All four units are ultimately destroyed by Eva-02. When killed, they explode violently despite their small size.

Mari later notes that Eva-13 does not have an AT Field. The purpose of the hoppers may be to remedy this deficiency. Eva-13 does not deploy any AT Field of its own, and any seen are solely due to the hoppers.
